indicators.name: dining table
indicators.rationale: The dining table is too large and lacks a built-in kitchen island, which is essential for daily cooking.

indicators.name: chairs
indicators.rationale: The chairs are not designed for kitchen use and do not provide ergonomic support for extended cooking tasks.

indicators.name: no stove or oven
indicators.rationale: The absence of a stove or oven makes it impossible to cook food in this space.

indicators.name: no sink
indicators.rationale: The lack of a sink means there is no water access for food preparation and cleaning.

indicators.name: no counter space
indicators.rationale: There is no hard work surface for food preparation.

reasoning_summary: This indoor scene is marginally adequate for daily cooking due to the lack of essential kitchen appliances and lack of hard work surfaces. The presence of a dining table and chairs suggests this space is primarily designed for dining rather than cooking.
